title: dryopteris wallichiana subsp. nepalensis
intro: Dryopteris wallichiana subsp. nepalensis, also known as the Himalayan lady fern, is a subspecies of fern native to the Himalayan region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and aiding in digestion. Herbal preparations of Dryopteris wallichiana subsp. nepalensis can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using them as a decoction to create teas, infusions, or tinctures.
